title: Silverstone Circuit
sub: F1’s spiritual home, built on a WWII airfield. Pancake-flat and ferociously fast, with the legendary Maggotts-Becketts-Chapel esses at its heart.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Quick Abbey, then the tight Arena loop (Village, The Loop). Braking and traction zones.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The signature stretch: Copse and the flat-out Maggotts-Becketts-Chapel esses. Where the lap is won.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: Hangar Straight into Stowe, the slow Vale braking, and the long double-apex Club onto the line.

pit: one of the longer pit lanes
tyreAlloc: Hardest in the range · C1 / C2 / C3
stratNote: High-energy corners (Maggotts-Becketts, Copse) push huge lateral loads through the tyres, so Pirelli brings its toughest compounds. Degradation drives the call.
spots: End of Wellington Straight + DRS 1. The classic late-brake lunge.
End of Hangar Straight + DRS 2. Slipstream then dive.
Secondary spot into the Arena loop on a good exit from Abbey.
otNote: DRS gives real chances, but following through the high-speed esses scrubs front tyres and dirty air bites, so passes mostly happen at the two straight-ends.
corners: Flat-out opener, ~290 km/h. Site of the chaotic 2022 first-lap pile-up.
Gentle left kink; cars rejoin from the pit exit here.
Heavy braking into a slow right, opening the Arena complex.
Slowest point on the lap, a 2nd-gear left hairpin.
Exit speed is everything: it feeds the long Wellington Straight.
Big stop at the end of Wellington Straight; prime overtaking spot.
A tightening ~180° right that wraps back toward the old pits.
Sweeps onto the National (old) pit straight.
~290 km/h commitment corner. The 2021 Hamilton–Verstappen 51G crash.
First half of the legendary high-speed sequence.
Rapid changes of direction at ~250 km/h; a true car test.
Final flick of the complex, launching onto Hangar Straight.
5th/6th-gear right at the end of the Hangar Straight.
Heavy braking zone, the lap’s second-slowest point.
Double-apex right that releases onto the start/finish straight.
geo: Silverstone sits on the former RAF Silverstone, a WWII bomber base opened in 1943. The layout traces the old perimeter roads.
A left-right-right-left sequence (T10–14) at 250–290 km/h. Among the greatest corner complexes in motorsport.
~65% of the lap is full throttle and top speeds near 330 km/h with DRS. Medium-high downforce.
The infield "Wing" houses the pit and paddock (2011). Mercedes’ Brackley base is barely 10 miles away.
